sections.title: Introduction
sections.content: The lesson opens with a motivational thought about perseverance: 'I will never give up until I basket the ball. Will you?'

sections.title: My Little Pictionary
sections.content:
1. award (v): a prize given to recognize an achievement.
2. technologist (n): an expert in any technology.
3. shock (v): cause someone to feel surprised and upset.
4. magic (n): tricks, such as making things disappear and reappear, performed as entertainment.
5. rub (v): move one object against another.

sections.title: Prose Summary: The Struggling Star - Morning Routine
sections.content: Farhan and his father (Abbu) have a daily morning routine. Farhan gets up, brushes his teeth, drinks his milk, and sits with his father to read the newspaper. One day, Farhan asks about the 'young leaders award' he sees in the paper.

sections.title: Prose Summary: The Young Leaders Award
sections.content: Farhan's father explains that the Young Leaders Award is given to only 17 people worldwide every year. It recognizes efforts taken to make the world a better place. Farhan is proud that an Indian won it. His father emphasizes that it is important not just to be proud of such news, but to learn from it.

sections.title: Prose Summary: Kartik's Early Life and Struggle
sections.content: The award winner is Kartik, a blind technologist. He studied at Delhi Public School in New Delhi. Though smart in all subjects, he loved Science the most. Despite scoring full marks in his 10th standard public exam, the Delhi government rules did not allow blind children to study Science in 11th and 12th standard.

sections.title: Prose Summary: Kartik's Perseverance (Campaigning for Science)
sections.content: Kartik refused to let his disability stop him. He believed everyone should be able to study what they like. He challenged the government and held campaigns. After nine long months of struggle, he not only got permission to study Science for himself but ensured that all blind students in India could study Science if they wanted. He became the first blind student in India to study Science. He worked hard and scored over 95% in his 12th board exams.

sections.title: Prose Summary: The IIT Struggle and Stanford
sections.content: Kartik's dream was to study in an Indian Institute of Technology (IIT). However, none of the sixteen IITs in India enrolled him because of his blindness, treating his talent unfairly. Again, he fought to change their minds. After many months, he succeeded, forcing the colleges to open their doors to blind students, eventually helping about 15 blind students study engineering there. However, Kartik did not study at IIT. Instead, he got admission to study computers at Stanford University in the USA, one of the best universities in the world.

sections.title: Prose Summary: Kartik's Vision and Father's Advice
sections.content: Kartik started two companies aimed at helping children with disabilities reach their full potential. He believes education and technology are crucial for children to learn and sustain themselves. He aims to ensure all 130 crore people in India reach their full potential. Inspired, Farhan promises to work hard to achieve his dreams. His father advises him that if we work hard, support will always come—sometimes from the outside, but mostly from within ourselves.

sections.title: Glossary
sections.content:
1. recognize: to know/accept someone or something.
2. empower: to give the authority or power.
3. campaigns: activities for some specific purpose.
4. potential: talent/ability.
5. sustain: keep going.
6. inspiring: to influence/attract.

sections.title: Let Us Build (Grammar: Gender of Nouns)
sections.content:
The gender of a noun indicates whether a person or animal is male or female.
- Masculine nouns: words used for men, boys, and male animals.
- Feminine nouns: words used for women, girls, and female animals.
Human Examples: grandfather/grandmother, father/mother, brother/sister, uncle/aunt, son/daughter, king/queen, prince/princess, hero/heroine, man/woman, sir/madam, waiter/waitress, bridegroom/bride, nephew/niece.
Animal Examples: bull/cow, rooster/hen, peacock/peahen, stag/hind, lion/lioness, stallion/mare, fox/vixen, ram/ewe, drake/duck.

sections.title: புத்தக மதிப்பீட்டு வினாக்கள் (Book Back Evaluation)
sections.content:
### A. Choose the best answer
1. Kartik is a ________. 
Ans: b) blind

2. The Delhi government did not allow blind children to study Science after ________. 
Ans: b) 10th standard

3. Finally, he got permission to study Science ________. 
Ans: c) after 9 months

4. He got an admission to study at Stanford University ________. 
Ans: a) in the USA

### B. Answer the following questions
1. What subject did he like the most? 
Ans: Kartik liked Science the most.

2. How many marks did he score in his 12th board exam? 
Ans: He scored more than 95% in his 12th board exam.

3. What was his dream? 
Ans: His dream was to study in an IIT (Indian Institute of Technology).

4. Is there something that you will struggle for? why? 
Ans: (Student's own perspective answer).

5. What was Farhan's father's advice to his son? 
Ans: Farhan's father advised him that if we work hard, there will always be support for us. Sometimes it comes from outside, but many times it comes from within us.

### Grammar - A. Match the masculine noun with the feminine noun
1. Son - daughter

2. Tiger - tigress

3. King - queen

4. Prince - princess

5. Hero - heroine

### Grammar - B. Change the masculine noun to feminine noun
1. My uncle presented me a watch. -> My aunt presented me a watch.

2. A young man was driving the car. -> A young woman was driving the car.

3. May I come in sir? -> May I come in madam?

4. The waiter took the order. -> The waitress took the order.

5. The rooster scratched the earth for worms. -> The hen scratched the earth for worms.

### Grammar - C. Complete each sentence using the following words (uncle, bride, bulls, niece, king)
1. Farmer John has four cows and two bulls.

2. The bridegroom and the bride entered the hall.

3. The king and the queen attended the court.

4. Charles greeted his uncle and aunt.

5. I played with my nephew and niece.

questions.question: What is Farhan's favourite part of the morning?
options: Reading the newspaper with his father
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Farhan and his father are reading the newspaper, the favourite part of his morning.'

questions.question: What does Farhan call his father?
questions.explanation: Farhan says, 'Abbu, what is this y-o-u-n-g leaders award?'

questions.question: How many people around the world receive the Young Leaders Award every year?
questions.explanation: His father says, 'It is given to only 17 people from around the world every year.'

questions.question: What is the profession of Kartik, the award winner?
questions.explanation: Father mentions: 'Kartik is a technologist who has not let his disability stop him...'

questions.question: What disability does Kartik have?
questions.explanation: Father clearly states: 'Kartik is a blind.'

questions.question: Where did Kartik do his schooling?
options: Delhi Public School in New Delhi
questions.explanation: The story mentions: 'Kartik studied in Delhi Public School in New Delhi.'

questions.question: Which subject did Kartik like the most in school?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Kartik did well in all subjects but he liked science the most.'

questions.question: Why couldn't Kartik study Science in 11th and 12th standard initially, despite scoring full marks in 10th?
options: His parents did not allow him
The school did not have a Science group
The government rule in Delhi did not allow blind children to study Science after 10th standard
He changed his mind and chose Arts
questions.explanation: Father explains: 'Actually, the government rule in Delhi did not allow blind children to study Science after 10th standard.'

questions.question: How many months did it take for Kartik to get permission to study Science after challenging the government?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'Finally, after nine months, he got permission to study Science.'

questions.question: Kartik was the ______ blind student in India to study Science.
questions.explanation: Father mentions: 'Like Kartik who was the first blind student in India to study Science.'

questions.question: What percentage of marks did Kartik score in his 12th board exam?
questions.explanation: The text states: 'His love for Science helped him get more than 95% in his 12th board exam.'

questions.question: What does IIT stand for in the story?
questions.explanation: Father explains: 'Indian Institute of Technology is one of the best colleges in our country!'

questions.question: How many IITs were there in India at the time of Kartik's struggle, according to the text?
questions.explanation: Father says: 'But none of the sixteen IITs in India enrolled him.'

questions.question: Approximately how many blind students were able to study engineering because Kartik made the colleges open their doors?
questions.explanation: The text says: 'This helped nearly 15 blind students to study engineering in these colleges.'

questions.question: Where did Kartik finally go to study computers?
questions.explanation: Father says: 'He got an admission to study at Stanford University in the USA.'

questions.question: What is the goal of the two companies Kartik started?
options: To help children with disabilities reach their full potential
To run campaigns against the government
questions.explanation: Father explains: 'He also started two more companies that aim at helping children with disabilities reach their full potential.'

questions.question: According to Farhan's father, if we work hard, support sometimes comes from the outside, but where does it mostly come from?
questions.explanation: Father says: 'Sometimes the support comes from outside, but many times it comes from within us.'

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'empower' as given in the glossary?
options: to give the authority or power
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'empower' as 'to give the authority or power'.

questions.question: Which glossary word means 'keep going'?
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'sustain' as 'keep going'.

questions.question: What is the meaning of the word 'inspiring'?
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'inspiring' as 'to influence/attract'.

questions.question: In grammar, words used for women, girls, and female animals are called _______ nouns.
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Feminine nouns are words used for women, girls and female animals.'

questions.question: What is the feminine gender of 'rooster'?
questions.explanation: The matching picture in the text shows 'rooster - hen'.

questions.question: What is the feminine gender of 'stag'?
questions.explanation: The textbook lists 'stag - hind' for animals.

questions.question: What is the feminine gender of 'stallion'?
questions.explanation: The textbook provides the pair 'stallion - mare'.

questions.question: Which of the following is the correct masculine-feminine pair for a fox?
questions.explanation: The textbook shows the pair 'fox - vixen'.

questions.question: What is the masculine gender of 'ewe'?
questions.explanation: The textbook lists the animal pair 'ram - ewe'.

questions.question: What is the masculine gender of 'duck'?
questions.explanation: The textbook lists the pair 'drake - duck'.

questions.question:
Match the following: 
1. Nephew
2. Waiter
3. Bull
questions.explanation: The correct feminine forms are Nephew-Niece, Waiter-Waitress, Bull-Cow.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Farhan felt that everyone should be able to study what they like.
Reason (R): The Delhi government rule easily allowed blind children to study Science.
options: Both A and R are true
Both A and R are false
A is true, but R is false
A is false, but R is true
questions.explanation: Farhan did feel everyone should study what they like. However, the reason is false because the government rule did NOT allow blind children to study Science initially.

questions.question: Life Situation: If a male waiter comes to your table, you call him 'Waiter'. What do you call the female person who takes your order?
questions.explanation: The grammar exercise changes 'The waiter took the order' to 'The waitress took the order'.

questions.question: Analogy: Hero : Heroine :: Sir : ?
questions.explanation: The grammar exercise pairs 'sir' with 'madam' (e.g., May I come in sir? -> May I come in madam?).

questions.question: Life Situation: You are at a wedding. You see the man getting married is called the bridegroom. What is the woman getting married called?
questions.explanation: In the fill-in-the-blanks exercise, it uses 'The bridegroom and the bride entered the hall.'

questions.question: What word is used in the text to describe someone who is an expert in any technology?
questions.explanation: The Pictionary defines 'technologist' as 'an expert in any technology'.

questions.question: How did Kartik's efforts affect other blind students regarding Science education in Delhi?
options: It made it harder for them
He made sure that all blind students have permission to study Science if they want
Only Kartik was allowed, no one else
It had no effect on others
questions.explanation: The text states: 'Not only that, he made sure that all blind students have permission to study Science if they want!'

questions.question: Why does Farhan's father say, 'Change takes time Farhan'?
options: Because it took Farhan a long time to get ready for school
Because it took 9 months for Kartik to get permission from the government
Because Stanford University is far away
Because reading the newspaper takes time
questions.explanation: When Farhan exclaims 'Wow! 9 months! It is so long!', his father replies, 'Change takes time Farhan. We should work hard and be patient...'

questions.question: Which word from the text means 'activities for some specific purpose'?
questions.explanation: The glossary explicitly defines 'campaigns' as 'activities for some specific purpose'.

questions.question: What is the feminine gender of a 'peacock'?
questions.explanation: The textbook shows the masculine-feminine animal pair as 'peacock - peahen'.

questions.question: Change the masculine nouns to feminine in this sentence: 'The king and the prince attended the court.'
options: The queen and the princess attended the court.
The queen and the heroine attended the court.
The madam and the princess attended the court.
The king and the queen attended the court.
questions.explanation: King becomes queen, and prince becomes princess.

questions.question: What does Farhan promise his father at the end of the story?
options: He will go to Stanford University
He will work hard and achieve his dreams, even without support
questions.explanation: Farhan says: 'I will also work hard and achieve my dreams, even if I don't always have the support I need.'

questions.question: What does the word 'recognize' mean as per the glossary?
questions.explanation: The glossary defines 'recognize' as 'to know/ accept someone or something'.

questions.question:
Assertion (A): Kartik was immediately accepted into Stanford University after his 10th standard.
Reason (R): The Indian IITs refused to enroll him because of his disability.
options: Both A and R are true
A is false, but R is true
A is true, but R is false
Both A and R are false
questions.explanation: Kartik was not immediately accepted into Stanford after 10th (he did 11th and 12th in India first). The reason is true; IITs did refuse him. So A is false, R is true.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct feminine word: Charles greeted his uncle and ________.
questions.explanation: Uncle is masculine, and the corresponding feminine noun is aunt.

questions.question: Fill in the blank with the correct masculine word: I played with my ________ and niece.
questions.explanation: Niece is feminine, and the corresponding masculine noun is nephew.

questions.question: According to the story, what two things are important to ensure children learn and sustain themselves?
questions.explanation: Father mentions about Kartik: 'He believes that education and technology are important to ensure children learn and sustain themselves.'

questions.question: How does the story define 'magic' in the pictionary?
options: A prize given to recognize an achievement
Tricks, such as making things disappear and reappear, performed as entertainment
questions.explanation: The Pictionary explicitly defines magic as tricks performed as entertainment.

questions.question: What is the masculine term for a female sheep (ewe)?
questions.explanation: The textbook identifies 'ram' as the masculine noun for 'ewe'.

questions.question: Analogy: Drake : Duck :: Lion : ?
questions.explanation: Drake/Duck is Male/Female. The female counterpart of Lion is Lioness.

questions.question: Life Situation: If you see a male deer with large antlers in the forest, you might call it a stag. What would you call a female deer?
questions.explanation: Based on the text's animal gender pairs, the feminine of stag is hind.

questions.question: What did the newspaper article Farhan and his father read talk about?
questions.explanation: The conversation starts with Farhan asking, 'Abbu, what is this y-o-u-n-g leaders award?'

questions.question: Based on Kartik's story, which quality best describes his character?
questions.explanation: The story highlights his continuous struggle against the government and IITs, demonstrating his perseverance.
